# Ch 3 — The Waterman — Citation Apply-Spec

**Date:** 2026-06-08
**Chapter (clean prose):** `manuscript/chapters/Chapter__3_TheWaterman.md`
**Class:** internal scaffolding (apply-spec only; the prose citation pass itself is end-user-facing and requires author ratification — do NOT edit the chapter unilaterally).

**Inputs merged:**
- `manuscript/ledgers/_CITATION_EVIDENCE_LEDGER_2026-06-04.md` (Ch3 block, lines 53, 81, 121)
- `manuscript/ledgers/_citation-evidence-detail_2026-06-04.json` (unit "Ch3": existing_citations + supported_claims + needs_research)
- `tools/audits/corpus-primary-source-register_2026-06-08.md` §1C (Chesapeake verified primaries), §2 (corrections), §6C (Colden/Moore/CBF folder mine)
- `research/outreach/subjects/colden/pre-publication-citation-verification-packet_2026-05-13.md` (12-quote primary-URL packet)
- `research/case-studies/chesapeake-fisheries.md`

**Status legend:** READY-TO-CITE = external primary source + URL in hand • NEEDS-PIN = source class known, specific document/URL not yet pinned • AUTHOR-CONFIRM = memoir/lived-experience, only the author can confirm • UNVERIFIABLE = no primary located • FRAMEWORK-OUTPUT = book's own construct, label-don't-source.

---

## B. Corrections (figures the primary source contradicts — reputational priority)

| Item | Correction | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **Menhaden cap date (ch line 115: "set ... in 2006 and reduced in 2017 to ... 51,000 metric tons")** | **WRONG as written.** The 51,000 mt cap is **ASMFC Amendment 3, adopted 2017 / implemented 2018** — NOT "set 2006, reduced 2017." Register §2.4 + §6C. The cut was ~41.5% (≈87,000 → 51,000 mt) on Colden's own Nov-2017 motion. **Fix the prose to drop "2006."** | ASMFC Amendment 3 <https://asmfc.org/news/fact-check/atlantic-menhaden-faqs/>; Anstead 2020 *Frontiers* 7:606417 |
| **Tangier "15 ft/yr" / "2 sq mi" (ch line 156)** | Peer-reviewed average is **~1.7 ft/yr** (0.5–1.3 m/yr) with **~1.2 sq mi remaining land**; "15 ft/yr" is USACE-via-press for the **exposed western shore**. Reframe as exposed-shore maximum, not Bay-average. Register §2.10. | Schulte/Dridge/Hudgins, *Sci. Reports* 5:17890 (2015) |

## E. Memoir / lived-experience — AUTHOR-CONFIRM (no external source applicable)

These are the narrator's first-person lived material; not externally sourceable, only author-confirmable. Not citation gaps.

- Author lives aboard a boat in Hampton; grew up near the bay; family from Fox Hill; grandfather worked at NASA Langley (ch lines 11, 22). **AUTHOR-CONFIRM** (grandfather-at-NASA-Langley corroborated by `project_authors_grandfather_nasa_inventor.md`).
- Bay-vs-ocean wave physics as lived knowledge; Fox Hill net-rebuilding mutual-aid mornings; "missing man" reorganization (ch lines 24, 28, 30). **AUTHOR-CONFIRM** (community-pattern testimony; wave-steepness physics is also a standard fetch-limited-shallow-water fact if ever challenged).
- **Biggie** (deceased oysterman, 30+ yrs): build, six-pack-of-Coke, eats oysters as he works, died before 1994 shad closure (ch lines 51–55). **AUTHOR-CONFIRM** — deceased-subject under courtesy-notify-family discipline (chapter line 50 carries the marker). The 1994 VA shad-closure *date* is READY/NEEDS-PIN per §A.
- **Unnamed Fox Hill crabber** (anonymized per consent discipline 2026-05-20; ch lines 141–150): mechanic shop, name on sign, crab pots behind locked fence. **AUTHOR-CONFIRM** — living-named-pending-consent; correctly anonymized; no citation applies.
- Author's own observations of greening water / lost grass-bed visibility since early-2000s (ch lines 135–136). **AUTHOR-CONFIRM** (lived observation; underlying eutrophication science is sourceable separately via CBP).

## F. Framework outputs — label, do NOT source

- The cost-severance / "renewable-turning-non-renewable" / "rents leaving the watershed" construct; the McDowell↔Bay "same mechanism" parallel; the "recovery without honest accounting" paradox framing (ch lines 14, 44, 198–226). **FRAMEWORK-OUTPUT** — the book's own analytic apparatus; label as framework-computed/derivation-shown, never an external cite. (Register §4 discipline.)
- Norway existence-proof preview (ch lines 232–236). **FRAMEWORK-OUTPUT / cross-chapter** — previewed here; canonical sourcing belongs to **Ch 4** (verify Ch 4 carries NBIM etc.). Do NOT assert as Ch 3's own unsourced facts. Colden peer-science (Colden, Latour & Lipcius, "Reef height drives threshold dynamics of restored oyster reefs," *MEPS* 582:1–13, 2017) is the load-bearing real-science anchor *under* the "renewable-past-regeneration" construct if the framework claim is ever challenged.

## Summary counts

- **READY-TO-CITE:** 24 (4 empirical figures + 1 disaster-declaration + 20 public-record named-subject quotes; the Tangier figure is READY-with-correction, counted once here)
- **NEEDS-PIN:** 14 (MSX/Dermo; shad closure; $3M VIMS study; Reedville largest-fishery + employment + geography; osprey; public-trust case reporters; CBP-1983/STAC review; dead zones; oyster-decline endpoint; J.S. Darling history; McMenamin patent; Crabtown)
- **CORRECTIONS (reputational priority):** 2 — (1) menhaden cap date "2006/2017" → **Amendment 3, 2017/2018** (drop 2006); (2) Tangier "15 ft/yr" / "2 sq mi" → reframe as exposed-western-shore max (~1.7 ft/yr avg; ~1.2 sq mi remaining).
- **AUTHOR-CONFIRM:** 5 clusters (memoir / Biggie / unnamed crabber / lived water-observation).
- **FRAMEWORK-OUTPUT:** 2 clusters (cost-severance apparatus; Norway preview → Ch 4).

## Highest-priority still-unsourced

1. **J.S. Darling & Son industrial history** (1884 / 160 shuckers / 200,000 bushels / 4-story shell mountains / menhaden factory) — ledger-flagged highest-risk; NO public citation; not in any folder. Hampton History Museum / Library of Virginia.
2. **Oyster historical decline endpoint** (15M → <200,000 bushels / 99%) — **not in any outreach folder**; Colden is not the source; keep web (Maryland Sea Grant/UMCES + NOAA). The <200K endpoint is the MED-confidence half — pin an MD DNR landings table or reframe to NOAA "1–2% of historic population."
3. **Watershed 64,000 sq mi** — READY but also **not in any folder**; lock the Chesapeake Bay Program watershed page so it isn't lost in the formalization pass.
4. **$3M VIMS Bay-menhaden study declined by VA GA (2023)** — pin the WHRO article + GA budget line.
5. **Public-trust case-law reporter citations** — verify the three Virginia Reports citations exactly as printed.

## Cross-spec flags

- **Name reconciliation:** chapter "Tanya O'Connor" vs consent-tracker "Tonya O'Connor" — confirm spelling + Petition 449 number before citing.
- **Menhaden "Bay cap" ambiguity (§6C item 18):** folders use "Bay cap" for both the striped-bass-era 2007 reference and the 2017 menhaden 51,000 mt cap — do NOT conflate when applying.
- **Adversarial balance (Pass 3.4):** §6C surfaced Menhaden Fisheries Coalition rebuttal (Oct 2025) + Ocean Harvesters response (Aug 2025) — available if the menhaden section needs detractor-side balance.
